behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff: three collectors must feed one summarization input payload. A unit test of a single formatter would miss wiring/aggregation bugs. Real OS/network is not required if source clients are stubbed at module boundaries, and this behavior does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real aggregation/orchestration modules with stub adapters for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log. Execute one report run and assert the exact payload sent to the summary-generation interface contains all three source sections with expected fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-pass: considered Unit, rejected because behavior explicitly requires cross-module data handoff from all three sources; considered System, rejected because no real network/OS behavior is needed to detect missing-source wiring.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is graceful-degradation behavior across collector, error handling, and report assembly modules. The key failure is fallback propagation, not single-function logic. Real infrastructure is not strictly required; an adapter stub raising timeout/connection errors is sufficient to expose whether orchestration continues and inserts the placeholder note.
behaviors.test_description: Use real report orchestration and rendering modules with three source adapters; force one adapter to fail (e.g., timeout exception), keep two successful. Assert run completes, report contains normal sections for available sources, and includes `data unavailable` for the failed section without process abort.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-pass: considered System due ‘unavailable source’, rejected because behavior text focuses on fallback output semantics, which are reproducible via in-process failure injection; considered Unit, rejected because failure requires interaction between fetch/error-handling/render modules.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (‘accurately represents’), which is a model judgment/output property. Deterministic stubs can only test wrapper logic, not whether real generation preserves key values; they can pass while real model outputs hallucinate/omit values.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against the real model with representative input datasets and assertions on factual fidelity (key metrics/issues/deploy counts preserved, no contradictions). Use repeated runs and threshold-based acceptance to account for non-determinism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-pass: considered Integration, rejected by behavior text ‘accurately represents’ because that requires evaluating real generated content, not just routing/retry plumbing; considered Workflow, rejected because this behavior targets one agentic generation step, not multiple agentic stages.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external I/O: HTTP/API fetching and actual email delivery to inbox. Mocks can hide SMTP/auth/configuration and end-to-end transport issues. The assertion is delivery outcome, not LLM quality, so Agentic is not required as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in an environment with reachable test instances (or staging equivalents) for data sources and mail service, sending to a real test inbox. Verify one run produces a full report email received by configured recipient with expected subject/body structure.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-pass: considered Integration, rejected because behavior explicitly requires ‘reaches ... inbox correctly,’ which needs real transport/inbox observation; considered Agentic, rejected because success criterion is delivery path, not model decision quality.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans network failure handling, filesystem persistence, and cross-run retry semantics. Real failure often appears across process/run boundaries and actual unreachable service conditions; pure in-process mocks can miss persistence/scheduling boundary issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script in a test environment where mail endpoint is intentionally unreachable; assert fallback file is written to disk. On a subsequent scheduled invocation (separate process/run) with mail restored, assert queued report is retried and delivered, and retry state is cleared/updated appropriately.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-pass: considered Integration, rejected because behavior text requires ‘next scheduled run’ plus unreachable service and local-file persistence across runs, which is a real infra/process-boundary failure mode; considered Workflow, rejected because this is reliability orchestration without multiple agentic decisions.
